explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while fats are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in B tier with an overall score of 40 from a base nutrition score of 40. Context barely changed the result.

topPros.title: easy to fit into familiar meals
topPros.explanation: That lowers friction for regular use.

topPros.title: can be a very convenient protein vehicle
topPros.explanation: Convenience is one of dairy’s strongest practical advantages.

topPros.title: slots into meals very easily
topPros.explanation: Practical versatility matters a lot for dairy.

topCons.title: fat quality varies wildly across dairy
topCons.explanation: Some dairy is clean, some really is not.

topCons.title: not everyone tolerates dairy well
topCons.explanation: That is a real use-case limitation.

topCons.title: processing changes the category a lot
topCons.explanation: The label alone does not tell the whole story.

closing.summary: Feta does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
Check that the final tier verdict feels editorially defensible.
